commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server end-of-run cleanup to schedule `nativeHookRelay.unregister()` on an unref'd 10-second timer instead of unregistering synchronously.

Reproducibility: yes. there is a concrete live command and instrumented before/after log path in the PR body, and current main source matches the synchronous unregister path. I did not rerun the live Codex harness during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A narrow repair can make the delayed unregister safe for reused relay IDs and add focused regression coverage before this PR is reconsidered.

target.title: fix(codex): defer native-hook-relay unregister to avoid cleanup race
target.body:
## Real behavior proof

**Source repro**: `runCodexAppServerAttempt`'s end-of-run cleanup at `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts:2758` (in current `main`) calls `nativeHookRelay?.unregister()` synchronously. Codex's hook subprocess spawned during the last tool dispatch may still be making its `nativeHook.invoke` RPC back to the gateway when this fires — when the RPC lands, the relayId is gone.

**Observed symptom** (this matches #73723's symptom report from a different submitter):

```
[ws] ⇄ res ✗ nativeHook.invoke 1ms errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=native hook relay not found conn=...
```

**Reproduction**: `2026.5.18 stable` + all 7 active local patches + clean `@openai/codex@0.130.0` binary. Probe an `eval-2` (codex harness) agent with a `python3 -c '...'` shell command (firewall has `explicit_deny` for python3 inline):

```bash
openclaw agent --agent eval-2 --message "Use exec_command to run python3 -c 'print(\"x\")'" --json
```

Expected: firewall blocks via `before_tool_call`. Observed: tool runs unblocked, zero `tool.exec` spans, zero `firewall_decisions` entries.

**Trace evidence** (instrumented `native-hook-relay-BGuJNohn.js` to log register/unregister/invoke lifecycle, instrumented codex binary to log dispatch_any + run_command spawn):

```
21:38:14.471Z UNREGISTER codex-fb6566dce... relays_size_before=0  (pre-register idempotent cleanup)
21:38:14.472Z REGISTER   codex-fb6566dce... relays_size=1
... probe runs, codex spawns relay subprocess during exec_command dispatch ...
21:38:45.684Z INVOKE     codex-fb6566dce... event=pre_tool_use found=TRUE relays_size=1  ← with this patch
```

vs. without the patch (also from instrumented runs earlier today):

```
01:24:26.715Z REGISTER   codex-08fa4130...
01:24:58.909Z UNREGISTER codex-08fa4130...  ← end-of-run finally block
01:25:00.104Z INVOKE     codex-08fa4130... found=false  ← 1.2s after unregister, lookup fails
```

The 1.2-second gap between unregister and late invoke is consistent. The 31-second gap between register and invoke is what made codex's PreToolUse hook timeout fire (default 5s, see followup discussion on #76793 about subprocess→gateway latency).

**Standalone evidence on the codex side**: built `codex 0.130.0` from source with `eprintln!` at `tools/registry.rs:362` (dispatch_any), `hooks/src/events/pre_tool_use.rs::run`, and `hooks/src/engine/command_runner.rs::run_command` (the spawn site). Codex correctly: discovers the hook (`handlers=2` `trust_status=Trusted`), dispatches `exec_command` through `dispatch_any` (`pre_tool_use_payload=Some("Bash")`), matches 1 handler in `pre_tool_use::run`, and `run_command SPAWNED pid_of_child=Some(...)`. The codex side has no bug. The relay subprocess is born and dies the way it's supposed to. The race is purely on the OC side — the registry is gone before the subprocess can call back.

## Problem

Codex-harness agents dispatched via the gateway can silently bypass OC's entire `before_tool_call` chain (firewall, MG, audit) for the closing tool call of every run. The bypass surfaces as `native hook relay not found` errors in the gateway WS log, but the failure is fail-open: codex's hook subprocess gets a non-2xx response, falls back to the unavailable-response shape, and codex proceeds with the tool dispatch as if no hook were configured.

This is independent of #73950's module-duplication fix. Even with the relays registry living on `globalThis`, the cleanup race in `run-attempt.ts:2758` still kills the registration before the in-flight subprocess can invoke. The two PRs are complementary, not competing.

## Change and value

`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts:2758` — defer the unregister with a 10s `setTimeout`, `unref()`'d to keep the event loop free. The 10s budget comfortably bounds the default 5s `hookTimeoutSec` from `buildCodexNativeHookRelayConfig` plus subprocess startup, WS connect, and gateway RPC roundtrip overhead. Late-arriving invocations land while the registration is still in the map.

Other `nativeHookRelay?.unregister()` sites in this file are unchanged:
- **Line 1351** (startup catch block) — fires before any tool dispatch, so no hook subprocess can be in-flight.
- **Line 2426** (turn-start failure cleanup) — same: before tool dispatches happen.

## Who's affected

All codex-harness agents dispatched via the gateway path (`openclaw agent --agent <id>` without `--local`). The bypass is for the LAST tool call of each run — not every call — because earlier calls' subprocesses get to land their invokes before the end-of-run cleanup fires. `--local` mode is unaffected (uses `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` intercepting at the JS layer before codex sees the call).

## Why now

Reproducible on `2026.5.18 stable`. The symptom has been visible to other submitters for weeks (#73723 since 2026-04-29, on `2026.4.26`). The root cause has not been pinpointed in the existing tracking, hence this PR.

## Implementation

Single 13-line change in `run-attempt.ts` — wrap the existing `nativeHookRelay?.unregister()` at the end-of-run finally block in a `setTimeout(..., 10_000).unref()`. The early-error-path unregister sites (1351, 2426) remain synchronous because no hook subprocess can be in-flight before tool dispatches begin.

## Out of scope

- **Latency mitigation**: PR #76793 ("Prefer direct native hook relay before gateway fallback") addresses the underlying latency that makes this race observable. They're orthogonal — even with the direct bridge always preferred, the cleanup race would still drop late-arriving fallback invocations.
- **Registry sharing**: PR #73950 ("share native hook relay registry") addresses the module-duplication theory. Also orthogonal — fixing the registry doesn't help if the relayId is unregistered before lookup.
- **Default `hookTimeoutSec` bump**: leaving as separate discussion (likely on #76793).

## Filing notes

Marked draft. Happy to add a regression test in `run-attempt.test.ts` that asserts the setTimeout is scheduled and unref'd (and that the relay survives a synthetic in-flight invoke), but I wanted to get the conceptual fix in front of maintainers first since this overlaps with two other in-flight PRs in the same area. Comments cross-posted on #73723, #73950, #76793.

Full investigation findings (instrumentation methodology, full trace, reproducer steps): writing to the openclaw downstream tracker; happy to attach here if useful.
target.author: kaspre
